title: Smoky Paprika Rosemary Dutch Stew
description: A floral and gooey Dutch dish featuring smoky paprika, rosemary, and perfect for a hangover cure, this recipe is both delicious and diabetic-friendly.

ingredients.name: 1 can (14 oz) condensed cream of mushroom soup
1/4 cup of low-sodium vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: Use water if sodium is not a concern

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons of smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Regular paprika can be used as a substitute

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary
ingredients.notes: Fresh rosemary can also be used, 1 sprig will suffice

ingredients.name: Pinch of salt and pepper to taste
ingredients.notes: Opt for low sodium salt if available

instructions: Place potatoes in a saucepan with chicken broth. Bring to boil and cook until tender.
Drain the potatoes, leaving about half an inch of liquid in the pot. Mash them gently with a potato masher or fork.
Mix condensed soup, smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and vegetable broth into the mashed potatoes. Mix well to combine.
If desired, stir in melted butter for added richness and flavor. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Pour mixture into a shallow baking dish (about 1 inch deep). Bake at 350°F for about 20 minutes or until set and bubbly.
Serve warm with sour cream or plain yogurt on top.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
